_metadata.dataset_name: CGST Input Tax Credit (ITC) Rules — Comprehensive
_metadata.source_authority: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC)
_metadata.governing_act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
_metadata.governing_rules: CGST Rules 2017 — Chapter V (Rules 36–45); CGST Act Sections 16–21
_metadata.last_amended: Notification No. 20/2024-Central Tax dated 08.10.2024; Finance Act 2023 (CSR amendment to Section 17(5))

rules.title: Entitlement to Claim Input Tax Credit
rules.description: Every registered person is entitled to take credit of input tax charged on any supply of goods or services or both to him which is used or intended to be used in the course or furtherance of business, credited to the electronic credit ledger.
key_principles: 'In the course or furtherance of business' is the fundamental test for ITC eligibility
ITC is available even if goods/services are 'intended to be used' — not just actually used
Credit is credited to the Electronic Credit Ledger (ECL) maintained on the GST portal
ITC can only be utilised to discharge output tax liability as per the order of utilisation under Section 49 of CGST Act

rules.title: Conditions for Claiming ITC — All Must Be Satisfied
rules.description: A registered person is entitled to ITC on an inward supply ONLY if ALL of the following conditions are simultaneously satisfied. These are overriding conditions ('Notwithstanding' language in Section 16).
conditions: (a) Must be in possession of a tax invoice / debit note issued by a registered supplier OR other documents prescribed under Rule 36
(b) Must have received the goods or services or both — for goods delivered in instalments, ITC available only on last instalment
(c) Tax actually charged on such supply has been paid by the supplier to the Government, either in cash or by utilisation of ITC
(d) Must have furnished the return under Section 39 (GSTR-3B) for the relevant tax period

Second Proviso: If payment is not made to the supplier within 180 days of the invoice date, ITC availed must be reversed (added to output tax liability along with interest) and may be re-availed once payment is made

Third Proviso (added via Finance Act 2022): Section 16(2)(aa) — ITC can be availed only if the details of invoice/debit note have been furnished by the supplier in GSTR-1 and appear in recipient's GSTR-2B

180_day_reversal.trigger: Failure to pay supplier within 180 days from invoice date
180_day_reversal.action: ITC must be reversed by adding to output tax liability in GSTR-3B
180_day_reversal.interest: Interest applicable under Section 50 from date of ITC claim
180_day_reversal.re_availment: ITC may be re-availed once payment is made; re-availment not time-barred under Section 16(4)

rules.title: Time Limit for Claiming ITC
rules.description: ITC in respect of any invoice or debit note cannot be claimed after the earlier of the following: (a) November 30 following the end of the financial year to which such invoice/debit note relates, or (b) date of furnishing of the relevant annual return.
conditions: Time limit: earlier of 30 November following end of FY or date of filing annual return
Example: ITC on an invoice for FY 2024-25 can be claimed up to 30 November 2025 or date of GSTR-9 filing, whichever is earlier

Exception (as amended by Finance Act 2022): Time limit of Section 16(4) does NOT apply to re-availment of ITC reversed due to non-payment under the second proviso to Section 16(2) — this can be re-availed at any time after payment
Amended from 'September 30' to 'November 30' by Finance Act 2022 (effective 01.10.2022)
ITC in respect of new registrations and deemed registrations is subject to separate time limits

rules.title: Apportionment of ITC — Business vs Non-Business Use
rules.description: Where goods or services are used partly for business and partly for other (personal/non-business) purposes, ITC shall be restricted to the portion attributable to business use.
conditions: ITC = (Business Use Proportion) × Total Input Tax Charged
Non-business use portion is ineligible — must be reversed
Similarly, where goods/services used partly for taxable (incl. zero-rated) and partly for exempt supplies, ITC restricted to portion attributable to taxable supplies
Method of calculation: Rule 42 for inputs/input services; Rule 43 for capital goods
Proportionate reversal to be done monthly (provisional) and finalised by September 30 of following FY

rules.title: Blocked Credits — ITC Not Available
rules.description: ITC is absolutely blocked (not available) for the following categories of goods and services, regardless of their use in business.
blocked_categories: (a)(i) Motor vehicles for transportation of persons having approved seating capacity of not more than 13 persons (including driver) — EXCEPT when used for: (A) making taxable supply of such vehicles; (B) transportation of passengers; (C) imparting training on driving; or (D) used as transport vehicle in further supply
(a)(ii) Vessels and aircraft — EXCEPT when used for making a taxable supply of such vessel/aircraft, transportation of goods/passengers, or training

(ab) Services of general insurance, servicing, repair and maintenance in so far as they relate to motor vehicles, vessels or aircraft referred to in (a) — EXCEPT if such vehicles used for exempt/taxable purpose above

(b) Food and beverages, outdoor catering, beauty treatment, health services, cosmetic and plastic surgery, leasing/renting/hiring of motor vehicles (for personal use), life insurance, health insurance — EXCEPT where the registered person is in the business of making such supply or where it is obligatory under law
(c) Works contract services for construction of an immovable property (other than plant and machinery) — EXCEPT where it is an input service for further supply of works contract
(d) Goods or services received for construction of immovable property on own account even if capitalised in books — EXCEPT plant and machinery
(e) Goods or services used for personal consumption
(f) Goods lost, stolen, destroyed, written off or disposed of by way of gift or free samples
(g) Tax paid under Section 74 (fraud, suppression), Section 129 (detention/release of goods) or Section 130 (confiscation) — ITC not available on such tax payments
(h) CSR expenditure under Section 135 of Companies Act 2013 — ITC blocked w.e.f. 01-10-2023 (Finance Act 2023 amendment)
rules.plant_and_machinery_exception: Plant and machinery means apparatus, equipment, and machinery fixed to earth by foundation or structural support — EXCLUDES land, building, civil structures, telecommunication towers, pipelines laid outside factory premises
rules.amendment_ref: Finance Act 2023 — CSR ITC blocked under Section 17(5); Finance Act 2021 — motor vehicle changes

rules.title: Documents Valid for Claiming ITC
rules.description: Input tax credit can only be availed on the basis of specified documents as prescribed under Rule 36(1).
valid_documents: (a) A tax invoice issued under Section 31 by a registered supplier
(b) An invoice issued by an unregistered supplier (self-invoice) under Section 31(3)(f) — for RCM supplies
(c) A debit note issued under Section 34 by a registered supplier
(d) A bill of entry or any similar document prescribed under Customs Act or Customs Tariff Act — for import of goods
(e) ISD invoice or ISD credit note issued under Rule 54(1A) — for ISD distributions
(f) Any document in lieu of invoice for banking companies, NBFCs, financial institutions under Rule 54(1) — subject to conditions
key_conditions: The document must be a valid document at the time of claiming ITC
Recipient must verify that the document number, GSTIN, and tax amount match the inward supply
ITC cannot be availed on proforma invoices, purchase orders, or documents that are not prescribed above

rules.title: ITC Restricted to GSTR-2B — No Provisional ITC
rules.description: With effect from 01.01.2022, a registered person can avail ITC only if the details of the invoice/debit note have been furnished by the supplier in GSTR-1 (or IFF) and appear in the recipient's GSTR-2B. Provisional ITC is no longer available.
conditions: ITC can only be claimed if the invoice/debit note appears in the recipient's GSTR-2B for that tax period or any prior tax period
If supplier has not filed GSTR-1 or the invoice is not reported, ITC cannot be claimed by the recipient (even if the invoice is valid)
History: From 09.10.2019 to 31.12.2019: 20% provisional ITC allowed; from 01.01.2020 to 31.12.2021: 10% provisional ITC allowed; from 01.01.2022: 0% — all ITC must appear in GSTR-2B
Exception: ITC from ISD distributions, import ITC (bill of entry), and ITC on reverse charge are not subject to GSTR-2B restriction (these appear via separate mechanisms)
GSTR-1A (amendment): ITC may be availed on amended invoices after supplier files GSTR-1A — credit flows into subsequent GSTR-2B

rules.title: Reversal of ITC on Non-Payment of Consideration to Supplier (180 Days)
rules.description: If a registered person fails to pay to the supplier the value of supply along with tax within 180 days from the date of the invoice, the ITC claimed must be reversed.
conditions: If the recipient does not pay the invoice value (including GST) to the supplier within 180 days from the invoice date, ITC availed on that invoice must be reversed
Reversal = ITC availed proportionate to unpaid amount
Reversal to be made by adding to output tax liability in GSTR-3B along with interest under Section 50
Interest runs from the date ITC was originally availed
Once payment is made (even after 180 days), the ITC can be re-availed
Reversal to be reported in GSTR-3B, Table 4(B)(2) as reversal due to Rule 37
This provision does NOT apply to Section 16(2) re-availment — once paid, ITC is re-claimable without time limit
For composition dealers: not applicable (they don't avail ITC)

rules.title: ITC Reversal When Supplier Does Not File GSTR-3B
rules.description: Inserted w.e.f. 26.12.2022. If a supplier has furnished invoice details in GSTR-1 but has not filed GSTR-3B (i.e., not paid tax) by September 30 of the following financial year, the recipient must reverse the ITC availed on such invoices.
conditions: Trigger: Supplier has reported invoice in GSTR-1/IFF but has not filed GSTR-3B by 30 September of the year following the financial year in which invoice was issued
Reversal deadline: The recipient must reverse the ITC by filing GSTR-3B on or before 30 November of that year
Reversal amount: Full ITC availed on all such invoices of the non-filing supplier
Interest applicable on reversed amount from original date of ITC availment under Section 50
Re-availment: Once the supplier files GSTR-3B and tax is paid, the recipient may re-avail the reversed ITC in GSTR-3B for any subsequent tax period — no time restriction on re-availment

rules.title: ITC for Banking Companies and Financial Institutions
rules.description: Banking companies, financial institutions, and NBFCs that opt not to comply with Section 17(2) proportionate ITC rules may follow the special procedure under Rule 38.
conditions: Such entities may avail 50% of eligible ITC each month on a self-assessment basis
The remaining 50% is not available
This is an option available in lieu of proportionate allocation under Rules 42 and 43
ITC on goods and services exclusively used for non-business/exempt purposes is still not available
ITC on inputs to capital goods used exclusively for business is available in full
The 50% restriction applies to common/mixed use inputs only
ITC availed must be credited to Electronic Credit Ledger under Section 41

rules.title: Procedure for Distribution of ITC by Input Service Distributor (ISD)
rules.description: The ISD must distribute eligible ITC to its recipient units in the same month it receives the credit, following the prescribed procedure.
conditions: (a) ITC available for distribution must be distributed in the same month it is received
(b) Eligible and ineligible ITC must be distributed separately
(c) IGST, CGST, SGST/UTGST credits must be distributed separately
(d) ITC available for distribution to a recipient = (Turnover of that recipient / Total turnover of all recipients in a State) × ITC to be distributed
(e) If a recipient has no turnover in the relevant period, their share is pro-rated from other recipients
(f) ISD cannot distribute more ITC than it has received
(g) ISD distribution reported in FORM GSTR-6 for each month
(h) ISD credit note for reversal where excess ITC was distributed
From 01-04-2025: Finance Act 2024 expanded ISD scope to cover inter-branch ITC on all input services (not just common services)

rules.title: ITC in Special Circumstances — New Registration, Voluntary Registration, Conversion from Composition
rules.description: ITC available to a registered person who migrates from non-registration to registration, or from composition to regular scheme, in respect of goods held in stock and capital goods.
conditions: New registration under Section 25(1): ITC on inputs held in stock/semi-finished/finished goods on date immediately before registration date — claim within 30 days of registration in FORM GSTR ITC-01
Voluntary registration: Same as new registration — inputs held in stock

Conversion from composition to regular: ITC on inputs in stock, semi-finished/finished goods, AND capital goods (net of 5% per quarter reduction) on date immediately preceding changeover date — claim in FORM GSTR ITC-01 within 30 days
Exempt supply to taxable supply: ITC on inputs/capital goods exclusively used for exempted supply now becoming taxable — claim within 1 year from invoice date
Capital goods ITC on composition-to-regular: reduced by 5% per quarter (or part of quarter) from original invoice date

rules.title: Transfer of ITC on Sale, Merger, Amalgamation, Demerger, or Transfer of Business
rules.description: Upon sale, merger, demerger, amalgamation, lease or transfer of business, the unutilised ITC in the Electronic Credit Ledger may be transferred to the transferee.
conditions: Transferor must file FORM GSTR ITC-02 electronically on GST portal with request to transfer unused ITC
In case of demerger: ITC to be apportioned in ratio of the value of assets of the new units as per demerger scheme
The transferor must attach a certificate from a Practicing CA or Cost Accountant certifying that the sale/merger/demerger has been done with specific provision for transfer of liabilities
GSTIN of both transferor and transferee must be in the same PAN — ITC cannot be transferred cross-PAN
Transferee's ECL will be credited after the transferor's GSTR ITC-02 is accepted
Unused CGST can only go to transferee's CGST credit; SGST to SGST; IGST to IGST

rules.title: ITC Apportionment — Inputs/Input Services (Taxable + Exempt Supplies)
rules.description: Where inputs or input services are used for both taxable (including zero-rated) and exempt supplies, ITC attributable to exempt supplies must be reversed using the formula in Rule 42.
procedure: Step 1: Identify inputs/input services exclusively used for taxable supplies (T1) — full ITC available
Step 2: Identify inputs/input services exclusively used for exempt supplies (T2) — ITC fully reversed
Step 3: Identify inputs/input services exclusively used for non-business purposes (T3) — ITC fully reversed
Step 4: Calculate Common Credit (C2) = Total ITC less T1, T2, T3, and inputs on which ITC is blocked under Section 17(5)
Step 5: ITC attributable to exempt supplies (D1) = (Aggregate exempt turnover / Total turnover in State) × C2
Step 6: ITC attributable to non-business use (D2) = 5% of C2
Step 7: Eligible common ITC (C3) = C2 - D1 - D2
Monthly: Reverse D1 + D2 provisionally in GSTR-3B
Annual Finalisation: D1 and D2 to be finalised by September 30 following end of FY based on actual annual figures; adjustment for difference between provisional and final
rules.interest_on_short_reversal: If actual annual reversal exceeds provisional reversals, excess must be added to output tax liability with interest under Section 50 from 1 April of the following FY

rules.title: ITC Apportionment — Capital Goods (Taxable + Exempt Supplies)
rules.description: Where capital goods are used for both taxable and exempt supplies, or for both business and non-business purposes, ITC on capital goods must be apportioned using Rule 43.
procedure: Capital goods exclusively used for exempt supplies or non-business purposes: FULL ITC reversal
Capital goods exclusively used for taxable (incl. zero-rated) supplies: FULL ITC available
Capital goods used for both taxable and exempt (common use):
— Total ITC on common capital goods = Tc
— Amount attributable to 1 month = Tc / 60 (ITC is spread over 60 months/5 years assumed useful life)
— Monthly reversal = (Tc / 60) × (Exempt turnover of month / Total turnover of month)
— Amounts to be accumulated monthly and finalised by September 30 of following FY

Subsequent sale of capital goods: ITC reversal required = Higher of: (a) ITC taken on capital goods less 5% per quarter (proportionate to quarters used); or (b) Tax on transaction value of capital goods at time of sale

rules.title: Reversal of ITC in Special Circumstances
rules.description: ITC must be reversed in certain specific circumstances including cancellation of registration, goods destroyed/lost, composition scheme migration, or supply becoming exempt.

circumstances_and_reversal.reversal_rule: ITC on inputs in stock, semi-finished/finished goods, and capital goods must be reversed — higher of: ITC on such goods OR output tax on transaction value; reversal in final GSTR-3B or GSTR-10 (final return)
ITC on inputs in stock, semi-finished goods, finished goods, and capital goods (net of 5% per quarter) on date of changeover must be reversed; in FORM GSTR ITC-03 within 60 days
ITC on inputs held in stock attributable to the newly exempted supply must be reversed

circumstances_and_reversal.circumstance: Goods lost, stolen, destroyed, written off, gifted, or given as free samples
circumstances_and_reversal.reversal_rule: Full ITC on such goods must be reversed as per Section 17(5)(f)
ITC reversal as per Rule 43 methodology — higher of reduced ITC or tax on transaction value

rules.title: Reversal of ITC — Additional Customs Duties (CVD/SAD) Under Legacy Law
rules.description: Rule 44A specifies the manner of reversal of ITC in respect of Input Tax Credits of Additional Duties of Customs (CVD) and Special Additional Duty (SAD) which were transitioned under the TRAN provisions.
conditions: Applicable to stock of imported goods held at the time of GST transition on which CVD/SAD was paid and transitioned as ITC
Where such goods are subsequently supplied as exempt goods, the transitional ITC must be reversed proportionately
Calculated as: ITC to be reversed = (Exempt supply value / Total supply value) × Transitioned CVD/SAD credit

rules.title: Conditions and Restrictions for Job Work ITC
rules.description: A registered person (principal) sending inputs or capital goods to a job worker can avail ITC on such goods subject to conditions under Rule 45.
conditions: Principal must maintain details of challans for goods sent to job worker
Details of goods sent to job worker must be declared in GSTR-1 (Table 9B) for each quarter
ITC on goods sent for job work: available to principal even though goods are not at principal's premises
Inputs to be returned within 1 year from date of sending to job worker (or removed to place of business/supply directly from job worker's premises)
Capital goods to be returned within 3 years from date of sending
If goods not returned within prescribed time: they are deemed to be supplied by principal on the date of sending — GST liability arises
Principal can supply goods directly from job worker's premises if job worker's premises are declared as additional place of business
Moulds, dies, jigs, fixtures, and tools sent to job worker: no such time limit applies

rules.rule_code: CGST Act Section 49 — ITC Utilisation Order
rules.title: Order of Utilisation of Input Tax Credit
rules.description: ITC must be utilised in a prescribed order to discharge output tax liability. Cross-utilisation rules apply.
utilisation_order: IGST credit can be used to pay: IGST first, then CGST, then SGST/UTGST (in any order)
CGST credit can be used to pay: CGST first, then IGST; CANNOT be used for SGST/UTGST
SGST/UTGST credit can be used to pay: SGST/UTGST first, then IGST; CANNOT be used for CGST
ITC must be fully exhausted before cash payment is made for the corresponding tax head
Cess credit can only be used to pay Cess; cannot be used for CGST, SGST, or IGST
electronic_ledger_types: Electronic Cash Ledger — holds cash deposited
Electronic Liability Ledger — holds tax demand payable

rules.title: ITC Linked to GSTR-2B — Mandatory Reconciliation
rules.description: From 01.01.2022, ITC claims must be supported by GSTR-2B. Registered persons must reconcile their purchase register with GSTR-2B before claiming ITC.
reconciliation_process: Step 1: Download GSTR-2B for the month from GST portal
Step 2: Match invoices in purchase register with invoices in GSTR-2B
Step 3: For invoices in purchase register but NOT in GSTR-2B — ITC cannot be claimed; follow up with supplier to file/amend GSTR-1
Step 4: For invoices in GSTR-2B but not in purchase register — verify genuineness of supply and document
Step 5: Claim only the ITC that appears in GSTR-2B
Step 6: Report mismatches in GSTR-3B Table 4 with appropriate coding (4D — ITC not available)
rules.gstr2b_auto_population: GSTR-2B is auto-populated on the 14th of each month based on suppliers' GSTR-1/GSTR-1A filed by the 13th. It is a static statement for each month — unlike GSTR-2A which updates dynamically.

rules.rule_code: CGST Act Section 18(6) read with Rule 44
rules.title: ITC Reversal on Sale of Capital Goods
rules.description: When a registered person sells capital goods on which ITC has been availed, the ITC must be reversed computed as the higher of two amounts.

reversal_formula.amount_a: ITC availed on capital goods less 5% per quarter (or part of quarter) of usage
reversal_formula.amount_b: GST payable on the transaction value (selling price) of the capital goods
reversal_formula.reversal_amount: Higher of Amount A or Amount B
reversal_formula.example: Machine purchased for Rs. 10L, GST paid = Rs. 1.8L (18%). Used for 6 quarters. Amount A = 1,80,000 - (1,80,000 × 5% × 6) = 1,80,000 - 54,000 = Rs. 1,26,000. Sold for Rs. 7.5L, GST on transaction value = 7,50,000 × 18% = Rs. 1,35,000. Reversal = Higher of Rs. 1,26,000 or Rs. 1,35,000 = Rs. 1,35,000
